output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford acquires land with an existing building for construction of a new factory. The purchase price is $60,000,000 face value, to be paid in 6 equal annual instalments of $10,000,000 each, starting at the end of the year of acquisition. Under IFRS (IAS 16), when payment is deferred beyond normal credit terms, the asset must be recorded at the present value of the payment obligation, with the difference between face value and present value recognized as interest expense over the payment period. The market interest rate of 6% is used to discount the payment stream. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land acquisition cost (site preparation costs are capitalized to land per IAS 16.16(b)). Present value calculation: sum of [10,000,000/(1.06)^i for i=1 to 6] = 49,173,245. The liability is recorded at the same present value amount.

output_entry_builder.reason: Ford acquires land (including existing building to be demolished for site preparation) for $60,000,000 face value, payable in 6 annual instalments of $10,000,000 starting at year-end. Under IFRS, the asset and liability are recorded at present value using the 6% market rate. PV calculation: 10,000,000/1.06 + 10,000,000/1.06^2 + 10,000,000/1.06^3 + 10,000,000/1.06^4 + 10,000,000/1.06^5 + 10,000,000/1.06^6 = 9,433,962 + 8,899,964 + 8,396,193 + 7,920,937 + 7,472,583 + 7,049,606 = 49,173,245. The difference between face value and present value ($10,826,755) represents implicit interest to be recognized over the payment period.
